I'm wired before the big presentation; every sense is sharp.
I've had three coffees and I can feel my heartbeat.
I'm in the zone and tracking every detail of the demo.
I'm at the party and reading the room hard, watching every face.
I'm meeting their family for the first time and every sense is on.
I'm waiting for them to arrive and I'm braced for it.
There's a storm coming and I'm watching the sky.
The dog perked up and now I'm scanning the yard too.
The wind shifted and I'm on my feet listening.
I'm watching the pan; it's about to burn.
The soup is at the edge of boiling over and I won't look away.
I'm timing the eggs to the second.
I heard a noise in the apartment and I'm sitting up listening.
I can't sleep; my mind is sharp and scanning.
I just woke up from a strange dream and every nerve is on.
I'm at the starting line; every muscle is ready to fire.
I'm climbing and every hold is getting my full attention.
I'm sparring and tracking my opponent's every shoulder twitch.
I'm driving in heavy rain and white-knuckling the wheel.
I'm in an unfamiliar neighborhood at night and walking fast.
I'm watching the platform for my train, ready to move.
The live broadcast is on and I'm glued to every update.
The score is close and I cannot look away.
The news is breaking and I'm refreshing the feed.
I heard the smoke alarm chirp and I'm tracking it.
The kettle is about to whistle and I'm two feet away.
I'm waiting for the timer to go and I cannot relax until it does.
